text: In Agile methodology, a development project is divided into short, focused periods called sprints. Each sprint typically lasts two weeks. The team starts each day with a quick standup meeting where members share progress and any problems. Work is managed using a backlog, which is a list of tasks to be completed. These tasks come from user stories, which describe features in simple language from the user's perspective. Agile uses iterative development, meaning the team builds and improves the product in small cycles. This allows for regular feedback and adjustments, ensuring the final result better meets users' needs.
